title: Trisetum altijugum

taxonomy: Trisetum altijugum is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Pooideae and the tribe Poeae. Further classification places it in the genus Trisetum. The subgenera classification is not available, but it is known to be a type of bunchgrass. Trisetum altijugum is native to the western United States and Mexico.

intro: Trisetum altijugum, also known as Three-awned Grass or Trisetum, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help in treating various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Trisetum altijugum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Trisetum altijugum is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, or used as a powder or extract, and is commonly used in the treatment of bronchitis, asthma, and other respiratory conditions.
